entry.title: Re: Custom Code decommission - Transaction usage
entry.content: Function Custom Code Decommission gets transaction usage data from ST03N on managed systems. So please logon production can run transaction ST03N to confirm whether the transaction usage can be recorded. If a transaction execution can not be recorded by ST03N, then it is out of the scope and Custom Code Decommission will also not be able to show its usage.

Please let me know if more detail is needed happy to discuss this further. 

Regards,

Antonis

entry.title: Introducing DelayCode: A Timing Delay Custom Widget for SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
The DelayCode custom widget is a custom timing delay component designed for integration with SAP Analytics Cloud. It provides a simple API for introducing delays, which can be useful in scenarios such as waiting for user interactions, pacing animations, or managing asynchronous operations.

Workflow

Usage

To get started with DelayCode, simply download the DelayCode.json file from the link below. Then, just follow the instructions provided to install DelayCode in your SAP Analytics Cloud environment.

GitHub Repository: https://github.com/SAP-Custom-Widget/DelayCode
Direct Download: https://sap-custom-widget.rohitchouhan.com/?dl=DelayCode

  • Download the DelayCode.json.
  • Go to your SAC Portal, select Stories/Analytic Application from the left sidebar, and then go to the Custom Widget tab.
  • Click on the + icon on the right side and select the CacheBuddy.json file that you downloaded.
  • You're done! You can now use it in your app.

Methods

The DelayCode widget exposes the following method:

delay

  • Description: Introduces a delay for the specified number of milliseconds. This method can return a boolean value indicating whether the operation was successful.

  • Parameters:

    • milliseconds (number): The number of milliseconds to delay before the method resolves. For example, passing 1000 will result in a 1-second delay.

  • Return Type: boolean

  • Usage Example:

// Logs a greeting message to the console on initialization
console.log("Hello There");
// Output: Hello There

// Introduces a delay of 10,000 milliseconds (10 seconds)
DelayCode_1.delay(10000);

// Logs a farewell message after the delay completes
console.log("Bye, See you");
// Output: Bye, See you (after 10 seconds)

Events

onTimeOut

This widget exposes a single event named onTimeOut, which serves as a determinative callback that is triggered upon the completion of the configured delay interval.

Conclusion

The DelayCode widget is a lightweight tool that integrates smoothly into the SAP Analytics Cloud environment, allowing developers to implement timing delays efficiently. Future versions may expand functionality with additional properties and event handling capabilities.

entry.content:
Hi @joaooliveiralopes 

As of release 2408, the generated BC app uses a transport selection bar by the ADT wizard, see also https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/how-to-activate-a-transport-request-selection-bar-for-the-rap-bo-of-a/ba-p/13637059

To enable this transport selection bar in your custom SAP Fiori elements app, you need to add a manifest annotation that is not yet publicly documented, but soon. Once the documentation is available, I will update this blog post.

In the meantime I would recommend you run the ADT wizard again and deselect the option "Enable Transport Selection Strip".

entry.title: xercesc_3_2::RuntimeException Error while loading SAP crystal report 2016
entry.content:
Persistent xercesc_3_2::RuntimeException when Loading Crystal Reports 2016 (32-bit) in MFC C++/CLI App (VS2019 32-bit)

Hello everyone,

I'm encountering a stubborn xercesc_3_2::RuntimeException when trying to load a Crystal Report (.rpt file) in my MFC C++/CLI application developed with Visual Studio 2019. I've tried extensive troubleshooting, and I'm hoping for some fresh insights, especially regarding this specific exception with matching bitness.

Problem Summary: When my MFC application calls m_report->Load(reportPath); (where m_report is a CrystalDecisions::CrystalReports::Engine::ReportDocument^ instance), an immediate Microsoft C++ exception: xercesc_3_2::RuntimeException is thrown. The debugger shows the exception originating deep within Crystal Reports' native components, likely during the XML parsing of the .rpt file.

My Environment:

  • Development IDE: Microsoft Visual Studio Professional 2019 (Version 16.11.x, latest updates applied).

  • Application Type: MFC Dialog-based application (C++).

  • Managed Code Bridge: C++/CLI Wrapper project (CrystalReportsWrapper.dll) exposing a System::Windows::Forms::Control-derived class (CrystalReportHostControl) to host the Crystal Report Viewer.

  • Target Framework: .NET Framework 4.7.2 (consistent across both MFC and C++/CLI projects).

  • Crystal Reports:

    • SAP Crystal Reports, version for Microsoft Visual Studio 2016 (design-time component).

    • SAP Crystal Reports runtime engine for .NET Framework (32-bit) (Crucial: confirmed 32-bit installation).

entry.title: Using Self Signed certificates with SAP Custom Domain service for Integration Flow Authentication
entry.content:
The SAP Custom Domain service allows you to use your own custom domain (e.g., yourcompany.com) for applications hosted on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), replacing the default SAP-provided URLs. It enhances branding, security (with TLS/SSL certificates), and user experience.

 In this blog we explain how to use SAP custom domain service with self-signed certificates for exposing integration flows deployed on to SAP Integration Suite runtime.

Later user can invoke the deployed integration flows endpoints with SAP Custom Domain Service endpoints with OpenSSL generated self-signed certificates as depicted in below diagram.

 

This blog we can breakdown in to below steps

  1. Domain Registered with Squarespace
  2. Azure DNS as the Authoritative DNS
  3. Using SAP Custom Domain Service configured with Registered Domain and SAAS Routes
  4. Generating Self-Signed certificates with OpenSSL and Add it to the Custom Domain Service Trust List.  
  5. Create Process Integration Runtime instance in BTP subaccount with service key based on Self-Signed certificate.
  6. Using SAP Integration Suite to create integration flows for testing which uses both Client Certificate approach and User Role approach.
  7. Invoke the integration flow endpoint with Custom Domain Service route and self signed certificates.

Domain Registered with Squarespace

We do a simple domain registration, which we may use later to be configured in SAP custom domain service for testing purpose.

Here I registered a domain called mayurpoc.org.

Azure DNS as the Authoritative DNS

Instead of using your domain registrar's default DNS servers, you delegate your domain's DNS resolution to Azure DNS.

Create an DNS Zone for the domain we registered in previous step and create a DNS Record, i.e.  CNAME record which maps a host alias subdomain to SAP Integration suite runtime Integration flow deployed endpoint host.   

Copy all the Azure DNS name servers from above portal and configure in the  Squarespace for the registered domain.

This means you update the nameservers (NS records) at your domain registrar to point to the Azure DNS name servers, this Azure DNS  provides advanced DNS features (like traffic routing policies, private DNS zones, etc.) that your registrar might not offer.

Using SAP Custom Domain Service configured with Registered Domain and SAAS Routes

Under both the reserved domains and custom domains add the domain we have registered in the first step.

Create SAAS route mapping, which maps the CNAME we created in Azure DNS records to the actual Integration Suite runtime Host URL.

Create CSR under server certificates in SAP Custom domain service, download it   and use the Certbot from letsencrypt to generate a server certificate for your custom domain.

certbot.exe certonly –standalone --preferred-challenges dns --csr C:\path\to\your\existing.csr

complete the DNS challenge when it asks to create DNS records in the Azure DNS Zone.

Upon successful validation of DNS records, certbot would generate the server certificate.

Upload the server certificate for the reserved domain and activate the same.

Generating Self-Signed certificates with OpenSSL and Add it Custom Domain Service Trust List.  

 Generating self-signed certificates with OpenSSL is a common task for development, testing, or internal-only applications where you don't need a certificate signed by a trusted Certificate Authority (CA).

This command generates both a private key and a self-signed certificate directly.

openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -nodes -keyout private.key -out certificate.crt -days 365 -subj "/CN=*.mayurpoc.org/O=SAP/OU=PM/L=Bengaluru/ST=Karnataka/C=IN"

create a TLS configuration in the SAP custom domain service and add this Self-Signed certificate to the Trust List.

This TLS configuration should be assigned to the server certificate activated for configured reserved domain.

Create Process Integration Runtime instance in BTP subaccount  with service key based on Self-Signed certificate.

Create Process integration runtime instance of integration-flow plan type. Create an service key of key type “External certificate”

Copy the Self-Signed CRT certificate content into External certificate and create key.

Using SAP Integration Suite to create integration flows for testing which uses both Client Certificate approach and User Role approach

First integration flow is based on User role and we intended to do simple authentication request from an external client , so this iflow gives back custom hardcoded response on HTTP GET request.

Second integration flow is based on Client certificate where we have to configure the SubjectDN and IssuerDN in the http sender channel.

Invoke the integration flow endpoint with Custom Domain Service route and self signed certificates

     Here I have used postman to invoke the deployed integration flows. I have converted OpenSSL self signed certificates to pfx format and imported it in postman settings.

   Invoked the integration flow endpoint for first use case, i.e. healthCheck Iflow configured with User Role. As you see invocation successful with Self-Signed certificate.

   Invoked the integration flow endpoint for second use case, i.e. healthCheck_ssl Iflow configured with Client Certificate. As you see invocation successful with Self-Signed certificate.

entry.title: Re: WRITE Data Store SAP entry ID is taking local variabl...
entry.content:
Since I had the same problem, here is the solution:
As described in https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/define-data-store-write-operations, if an entry ID is left empty, the system first checks whether the header SapDataStoreId is set. Among other things, this is also set by reading a local variable.
If you like the system to generate an entry ID for the data store operation, remove the header SapDataStoreId before the data store write step and leave the Entry ID field in the data store empty.

entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Build Code – June 2025 Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Greetings and welcome to the latest edition of our SAP Build Code release communications blog post! It’s June and we cross the halfway mark of the year. It’s the perfect time to introduce some exciting new features we've added over the past few months.

This update features a range of exciting new capabilities, such as the Joule-based SAP HANA Application Migration Assistant and the SAP HANA Cloud SQL generation via Joule with custom data models. In addition, we collected many informative blog posts delving into various aspects of SAP Build Code.

To explore these enhancements in depth, check out the details of what's new!

 

Joule-Based SAP HANA Application Migration Assistant

The Q2-2025 release introduces the new Joule-based SAP HANA Application Migration Assistant, an extension now available in SAP Build Code.

This enhanced tool extends the capabilities of the existing database migration assistant by automating the conversion of service layer that includes XSJSLIB, XSODATA, and XSJS artifacts from XS classic or XS advanced into modern SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP)-based services aligning with the SAP BTP Developer's guide.

Using generative AI, it simplifies service layer migration when customers choose "XS Classic to CAP" or "XS Advanced to CAP" templates.

The SAP HANA Application Migration Assistant

Selecting to migrate the service layer

Together, these tools reduce manual effort, accelerate modernization, and help businesses adopt modular, cloud-native architectures empowering faster innovation and effective transformation.

 

SAP HANA Cloud SQL Generation via Joule with Custom Data Models

The SAP HANA Cloud SQL generation feature, which was introduced in late 2024, has been updated to not only support generating standard SQL statements for public monitoring views, but also for custom data models from a customer SAP HANA Cloud instance. Since the feature requires sending database object metadata (i.e. SQL create statements) to a large language model (LLM) it must be enabled manually after verifying that sending the metadata is uncritical. To do so, the setting “SAP HANA Database Explorer: Use Object Definitions For SQL Generation” must be checked in the SAP Build Code settings.

Selecting “SAP HANA Database Explorer: Use Object Definitions For SQL Generation”

Once the setting is enabled, the SQL statement generation feature in the SAP HANA Cloud SQL console in SAP Build Code will take the existing database objects into consideration.

SQL statement generation

By default, only objects in the current schema are considered for the SQL generation. If additional schemas should be considered, this needs to be configured in the setting “SAP HANA Database Explorer: Schemas For SQL Generation”.

Providing a list of database schemas for AI-powered SQL generation

The database object metadata is stored inside the SAP Build Code dev space for a period of 7 days. After that period, the metadata information is automatically deleted and will be recreated on demand if it is needed again. The retention period can be configured via the setting “SAP HANA Database Explorer. Use Objects Definition For SQL Generation: Retention Time”.

During the SQL statement generation, the prompt along with suitable additional information from the database object metadata and from the SAP HANA Cloud documentation is sent to the LLM. With this information as the context the LLM can generate syntactically valid SQL statements that can be executed on SAP HANA Cloud. As with all AI-generated information users should check the statements carefully before executing them to avoid unintended side-effects.

With this improvement the SQL generation capabilities have become significantly more powerful and provide more value, especially for casual users. Of course, if required, the syntax can still be conveniently accessed via the syntax help panel in the SQL console for further information and fine-tuning.

 

The SAP Build Lobby now Supports the Creation of SAP HANA Cloud Intelligent Data Applications

From the SAP Build Lobby, you can centrally create new projects or access and manage your already existing projects. You can create new projects from scratch or use the "Clone from Git" or "Add from Dev Space" functionality to work with your existing projects. The creation via the SAP Build Lobby now also supports the creation of SAP HANA Native Applications.

To do so, select “Create”. Then choose “Application” as Objective and “Backend” as Category. You can now select “SAP HANA Cloud Intelligent Data Applications” as Type. Once your new SAP HANA Cloud Application project has been created you can open the project by selecting the corresponding link from the SAP Build Lobby.

Selecting to create a backend application of type “SAP HANA Cloud Intelligent Data Applications”

 

Further readings & Events

Discover What's New in Mobile Development Kit Client 25.6

We're excited to announce the newest release of the mobile development kit (MDK), packed with powerful updates that will elevate your mobile development projects. Discover new functionalities, such as support for grouping in object table control and lock and wipe settings in the MDK Client, among many other improvements.

Dive into the details of these latest enhancements and see how they can benefit your projects by visiting Jitendra Kansal’s blog post: What’s new in mobile development kit client 25.6.

 

SAPUI5 is Leading the Way in Accessibility with WCAG 2.2

Accessibility is key to using SAP's business solutions. By integrating the latest standards, we make sure our solutions are inclusive and cater to the diverse needs of our global user base. Developers creating apps with SAP Build and SAPUI5 can now benefit from the latest Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG). For more information, please visit the following link: SAPUI5 is Leading the Way in Accessibility with WCAG 2.2.

 

Simplify SAPUI5 Code Extensions with the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard

SAP S/4HANA Cloud has introduced the new SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, a unified tool that consolidates multiple extension options for a streamlined experience. You can now launch the wizard directly from a running SAP Fiori app, simplifying the extension process significantly. The latest update to the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard also introduces the capability to create an SAPUI5 adaptation project as a User Interface Extension.

Read more about this innovation in Oliver Graeff’s SAP community blog. 

 

Announcement: Removal of the Belize Theme of SAP Fiori

If you are working with the Belize theme of SAP Fiori you should read the following to learn about it’s removal and how to handle custom themes based on Belize: Announcement: Removal of Belize Theme of SAP Fiori.

 

UI5 Linter Now Includes an Autofix Feature!

UI5 Linter is your go-to tool for ensuring your SAPUI5 project adheres to best practices and replaces deprecated APIs. We’re thrilled to announce a significant enhancement in UI5 Linter v1.12.0: the initial version of an autofix feature! Now, you can automatically apply fixes to your code by simply using the "--fix" argument.

Excited to see how the UI5 Linter autofix feature can streamline your development process? Give it a try! For more details, read the following blog post: Migrate Your Code with UI5 Linter: Autofix Has Landed.

 

Code Connect 2025

One week, one location, three great events at Code Connect 2025: UI5con, re>≡CAP, and HANA Tech Con! Whether you are an experienced developer or new to SAP, join us in St. Leon-Rot, Germany, or online July 08-10 and connect, learn, & innovate.

 

For further information on new features, please check our roadmaps: roadmap for SAP Build Code, SAPUI5 Road Map. And have a look at What's New in SAP Build Code, the What's New Viewer for SAPUI5 and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Release Notes. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates for SAP Build Code: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding! 😊

entry.title: Unable to use reference field in bank statement memo line
entry.content:
Short describtion:

Hello,

when uploading the bank statement, we are searching for the reference field in the sales order in the memo line. It doesnt work because the reference field has too less signs. We need at least 20. But the field just provides 16. Do we have a alternative field to be filled?

Kind Regards

 

Long Describtion:

Hello,

the process is as follows: We create a sales order. In the sales order we enter a number in the reference field. We need it to later find the right open item. We post the sales order. Then we create a customer invoice. The customer invoice also generates an open item. The open has to be paid. The open item contains the reference field from the sales order. 

Then we upload the bank statement via app Reprocess Bank statement items. The bank statement position should clear the open item. In the memo line should be the same number as in the reference field. This memo line will be processed via an processing instruction. The problem is: The number in the memo line contains 20 field and the number in the reference field contains 16 fields. We need at least 20 signs for the reference field. Can you help us with an alternative?

Keep in mind: It is SAP Public Cloud!

Kind Regards

entry.title: Re: SO is confirmed when PO is created, not confirmed
entry.content:
Hi,

To change the behavior back to the original setting where the sales order is confirmed only when the related purchase order (PO) is confirmed, you will need to adjust the configuration settings related to the output determination for sales orders and purchase orders. This involves setting up the output parameter determination to ensure that the sales order confirmation is triggered by the PO confirmation rather than the PO creation.

Steps to Configure:

  1. Access Output Parameter Determination: How to generate purchase order output on change of… - SAP Community

    • Log into your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.
    • Navigate to the ‘Output Parameter Determination’ app.

  2. Modify Determination Rules:

    • Select ‘Show rules for: Sales Order’ and ‘Determination Step: Output Type’.
    • Ensure that the output type for sales order confirmation is set to trigger based on the PO confirmation status rather than the creation status.

  3. Adjust Output Type Settings:

    • Verify that the output type settings for sales orders are configured to wait for the PO confirmation.
    • You may need to consult with your SAP administrator or refer to specific SAP documentation for detailed steps on adjusting these settings.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Difference in VA05 output and BAPI_SALESORDER_GETLIST...
entry.content:
Hi,

The discrepancy between the number of open orders shown in the VA05 report and those fetched using the BAPI_SALESORDER_GETLIST Sales - Display Sales Orders - VA03 function module could be due to several reasons:

  1. Selection Criteria: The VA05 report and the BAPI function might be using different selection criteria or filters. Ensure that both are configured to use the same criteria, such as order status, date range, and customer details.

  2. Data Visibility: There might be differences in data visibility settings. The VA05 report might include orders that are not visible to the BAPI due to authorization or data restrictions.

  3. Data Synchronization: If the system is not synchronized, there might be a delay in data updates between the report and the BAPI function.

  4. Custom Enhancements: Check if there are any custom enhancements or user exits affecting the VA05 report or the BAPI function, which might lead to differences in the data fetched.

To resolve this issue, you should compare the selection criteria and settings used by both the VA05 report Sales - List Sales Orders - VA05 and the BAPI function. Additionally, verify any customizations or data visibility settings that might affect the results.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Movement Type to Good Issue Block Stock with Profit S...
entry.content:
Hello @imel_99 

Thank you for your question. Standard movement types like 291 (Goods Issue to Blocked Stock) typically do not carry Profitability Segment fields by default in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This is because goods issued to blocked stock are considered non-valuated and generally excluded from CO-PA-relevant processes.

Profitability Segment fields appear in processes affecting inventory valuation or consumption relevant for profitability reporting, such as movement types 201 (Goods Issue to Cost Center) or 601 (Goods Issue for Sales Delivery).

Movement type 291 moves stock to blocked status, but does not trigger a CO-PA posting in standard setup.

Recommendation

  • Review available movement types via SSCUI or Configuration Activities in the Manage Your Solution App under Inventory Management to confirm if other movement types support your scenario.
  • In the Public Cloud, extensions are limited. You may explore Custom Fields and Logic App for minor enhancements, but modifying CO-PA assignments for goods movements is usually restricted.
  • If tracking profitability is mandatory when posting to blocked stock, consider a process redesign, like issuing to an alternative account assignment that captures the profitability segment.

Useful References;

 SAP Help Portal – S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition- Goods Movement

SAP Help – Profitability Analysis (CO-PA) Overview

 KBA 3216727 – Goods Movements in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Login)

 SAP Learning – Inventory Management and Goods Movement in S/4HANA Cloud

Let me know if you need further assistance

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Movement Type to Good Issue Block Stock with Profit S...
entry.content:
Hello @Chuma 

The 291 movement type is an issue from blocked stock, not to blocked stock.

If a material stock is valuated then 291 issue is valuated too. Valuation doesn't really depend on movement types. Thus you can't say: "This is because goods issued to blocked stock are considered non-valuated"

If you consume blocked stock, then you incur the stock's costs/value. That definitely influences profitability - the more stock is consumed, the less the profitability. Thus, it should be captured by CO-PA, which is Profitability Analysis. Without that profitability analysis is skewed.

KBA 3216727 is "After doing a Save As in Web Inteligence, the browser tab name of the report does not change". It is not about movement types in S/4HANA public cloud.

One of your recommendations is: "If tracking profitability is mandatory when posting to blocked stock, consider a process redesign, like issuing to an alternative account assignment that captures the profitability segment." How do you see that in details? If S/4HANA public cloud doesn't allow to modify 291 configuration to add CO-PA postings, how do you propose to use "an alternative account assignment" for 291?

To me it simply looks like a missing feature - CO-PA in S/4HANA public cloud is blind to issues from blocked stock - without a decent workaround.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: SAP facts
entry.content:
Привіт, SAP Community Kyiv!

Як ми вже згадували раніше, SAP Україна відзначає своє 30-річчя, тому сьогодні хочемо поділитися з вами кількома цікавими фактами про SAP, які демонструють справжній масштаб впливу компанії. Деякі з них можуть здивувати навіть досвідчених SAP-івців🤫

SAP буквально керує світовою економікою

  • Чи знали ви, що клієнти SAP представляють аж 84% світової економіки? Це не просто цифра – це свідчення того, наскільки глибоко програмне забезпечення SAP інтегроване в глобальні бізнес-процеси.
  • Ще більш вражаюче: 98 зі 100 найбільших компаній світу використовують рішення SAP. А серед компаній зі списку S&P 500 (це список 500 найбільших американських компаній за вартістю) цей показник становить 98%! Фактично, майже кожна велика компанія, яку ви знаєте, є клієнтом SAP.

SAP домінує в кожній галузі

🚗10 з 10 найбільших автомобільних компаній працюють з SAP.

🏦10 з 10 найбільших банків світу довіряють SAP.

🍔10 з 10 найбільших компаній харчової промисловості використовують SAP.

💻10 з 10 найбільших виробників технологічного обладнання – клієнти SAP.

🎬9 з 10 найбільших медіа та розважальних компаній обрали SAP.

💾9 з 10 найбільших компаній у сфері програмного забезпечення також використовують SAP (іронічно, чи не так?).

SAP любить не лише гігантів

  • SAP – це не лише для великих корпорацій: 40% компаній середнього бізнесу теж обрали SAP.
  • 6 з 10 найбільших компаній середнього ринку працюють з SAP.

Якщо подумати, це означає, що:

  • Майже кожен продукт, який ви купуєте, був вироблений, розповсюджений або проданий за допомогою систем SAP.
  • За ваші банківські операції, скоріше за все, відповідає SAP.
  • Ваша їжа, одяг, транспорт, електроніка – все це вже десь пройшло крізь «магію САП».

А яка статистика про SAP здивувала вас найбільше? Діліться своїми думками в коментарях👊🏻

Ці та ще більше цікавих фактів тут.

#SAPUkraine30 #SAPCommunityKyiv #SAPFacts #SAPCommunity# #SAP

entry.title: Creating Mashup to Show SAP S/4HANA Addresses in SAP Service Cloud Version 2 for Utilities Add-on
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Service Cloud Version 2 does not use validity on addresses, but for the SAP S/4HANA Utilities this is one of the topics that are available and sometimes needed for an agent to see.

There exists an unpublished API in SAP Service Cloud Version 2, that can be used to retrieve and show this information. In this blog, I show how to add this information as a separate tab on the Contract Account view.

The result from the below looks like this:

The steps used to create this function:

  1. Create a mashup (HTML page) to retrieve and display the information
  2. Configure mashup in SAP Service Cloud Version 2
  3. Add this mashup as a new tab in SAP Service Cloud Version 2.

I will assume that all the standard IFlows are activated. If you need some further information on how to activate IFlows, please have a look at this blog: Mass Deploy of iFlows.

 

Technical components used:

SAP Service Cloud Version 2 API. I have added an example including the filter options. The actual object ID should of course be one from your system.

{{HOSTNAME}}/{{API_BASEPATH}}/{{V1}}/utilities-common-integration-service/addresses?$filter=addressType eq F and objectId eq 7000003995 

Data Integration Flow

Get Utilities Object Address

S/4HANA Function Module

ISU_C4C_SAP Service Cloud Version 2_OBJECT_ADDRESS 

 

Creating the mashup

This is a simple HTML page showing a table of the addresses. The call to the API and passing the data to the table is done using JavaScript.

When calling the SAP Service Cloud Version 2 API, it is good practice to authenticate the call using an API user. For more details, please have a look at this note: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003417189.

To make the code example as simple as possible, I have included both CSS styling as well as the JavaScript code in the HTML page.

The HTML page has 3 main parts:

  1. Parse out the Business Partner ID, needed for the API call
  2. Make the call to the API
  3. Format the table with data from the response.

Parsing out the Business Partner ID from the URL call

When the HTML page is called from SAP Service Cloud Version 2, the parameters are passed as search parameters. When we later configure the mashup in SAP Service Cloud Version 2, we provide the parameter name that is used, here I call this ‘par1’.

As a side note, it is of course possible to pass several parameters from SAP Service Cloud Version 2, but for this use case we only need one.

With the above code, we have the Business Partner ID in the parameter ‘par1’. Linking Business Partner ID to ‘par1’ is done when the mashup is configured in SAP Service Cloud Version 2 – see later.

The next step is to call the API.

 

Calling the SAP Service Cloud Version 2 API

The only parameters I use are the Business Partner ID and the address type. The Business Partner ID is passed as a parameter from the Contract Account view and the address type is passed as a fixed value 'F'.

I use basic authentication and pass the username and password of the API user.

 

 

 

Format the response from S/4HANA.

For this purpose, the addresses we need are stored in ‘bpAddresses’, below a screenshot from the console in the browser:

 

Since this is an array, we will loop over this and create a line in the table for each occurrence.

 

Nothing more is needed to get the data and display this on a page.

The next step is to add this mashup in SAP Service Cloud Version 2.

 

Configure the mashup in SAP Service Cloud Version 2.

In SAP Service Cloud Version 2 under settings:

Settings -> Mashup Authoring, the newly created HTML page is added.

There are only 3 fields needed:

  • Mashup name (1)
  • URL for the mashup (2)
  • Name of parameter passed (3). This has to match the parameter we look for when parsing out the parameters in the JavaScript code.

 

 

When these things are filled in, it is possible to see a preview of the view.

 

 

For the preview option no Business Partner ID is passed, and the table is empty. If you would like to verify that it looks good with data, you could add a default Business Partner ID in your code.

 

Using adaptation to add the mashup to a tab

The mashup needs to be added a place where the Business Partner ID can be passed as a parameter. Here this is done from the Contract Account view.

 

 

 

One point on passing the Business Partner ID. If the mashup was added as a tab in Agent Desktop, there are no single parameter for the Business Partner ID. In this case you would have to pass both the Business Partner ID for the Individual Customer and Account, always passing two parameters.

In the HTML code, logic is then used to select the parameter with a value and then pass this to the API. Only one of the two parameters would ever be populated, since a Business Partner is either an Individual Customer or an Account.

 

 I hope the above showed how simple it is to create a mashup and add this to SAP Service Cloud Version 2. If you have further questions, please ask.

 

Appendix

Below a copy of the complete source code:

 

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
 
<head>
<title>Get Business Partner Addresses</title>
<meta charset="UTF-8">
 
</head>
<style>
table,
th,
td {
border: 1px solid;
border-collapse: collapse;
padding: 15px;
}
</style>
 
<body>
<h2>Business Partner Addresses 2</h2>
 
<table id="myTable" class="table1">
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Address</th>
<th>Valid from</th>
<th>Valid to</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody id="testBody"></tbody>
</table>
 
<script>
//   Get Business Partner ID, passed from V2   
var parameters = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
var par1 = parameters.get('par1');
 
console.log(par1);
 
if (par1) {
x = "Good day";
}
else { par1 = 'US00002808'}
 
console.log(par1)
 
let username = 'xxxxx';
let password = 'yyyyy';
 
let auth = btoa(`${username}:${password}`);
 
let urltenant = ' https://zzzzzzzzzzzzz/sap/c4c/api/v1'
let urlservice = '/utilities-common-integration-service/addresses?$filter=addressType eq F and objectId eq '
// par1 = 'US00002808'
let url = urltenant + urlservice + par1;
 
fetch(url, {
headers: {
'Authorization': `Basic ${auth}`
}
})
.then((response) => {
if (!response.ok) {
throw new Error('Network response was not ok');
}
return response.json();
})
.then((data) => {
console.log(data);
 
const table = document.getElementById('testBody')
data.value[0].bpAddresses.forEach(item => {
let row = table.insertRow();
let addressDescription = row.insertCell(0);
addressDescription.innerHTML = item.addressDescription;
let From = row.insertCell(1);
From.innerHTML = item.validFromDate;
let To = row.insertCell(2);
To.innerHTML = item.validToDate;
});
 
})
.catch((error) =>
console.error('There was a problem with the fetch operation:', error),
);
 
</script>
</body>
 
</html>

entry.title: Re: Guidance Required on Enabling DTO for ADSOs with Non-...
entry.content:
Hi,
as mentioned already, DTO does not have to be managed by a time field, any key field is suitable. 0CAL/0FISC Time Characteristics are recommended, as they can prune based on the SAP time hierarchy and that is great for performance reasons.
If these fields are not in the key, you can evaluate first to put the whole ADSO into WARM. On object level there is no need for a partition criteria. if performance expectations are met for these ADSOs, then that is the easiest approach.

Unless consider, that changing key fields and/or partitions most likely result in expensive changes and risky remodeling runs. Those are best to be avoided...

KR Frank

entry.title: Wrap-Up SAP MaxAttention IWS 'Transform your Business with SAP Business AI' (Virtual, June 18, 2025)
entry.content:
Virtual

June 18, 2025

Agenda

Slides & Recording

Dear Valued Customers,

The SAP MaxAttention Innovation Workshop “Transform your Business with SAP Business AI – From innovation to impact” held on June 18, 2025, brought together industry leaders and SAP experts to explore how SAP Business AI can transform enterprises.

The event opened with a keynote by Dr. Walter Sun, who emphasized SAP’s Business AI strategy. He introduced Joule and AI Agents as the next-generation tools that revolutionize user experiences by automating tasks, providing intelligent recommendations, and enhancing decision-making. A lot of customer participants actively participated in the session via questions often focusing on Joule availability and capabilities.

A major highlight of the workshop was the session on customer use cases, where leaders from JCI and GE Aerospace shared real-world success stories of AI adoption. These sessions demonstrated how organizations are operationalizing AI across key business functions with the support of SAP MaxAttention services.

Attendees gained insights into best practices for AI adoption, from ideation and data preparation to model integration within SAP landscapes. This segment underscored the importance of expert guidance and structured methodologies in accelerating AI-driven transformation.

The workshop also delved into the evolution of Joule and the rise of Agentic AI, with a focus on tools like Joule Studio that accelerate and democratize AI development. The integration of Knowledge Graphs and BDC innovations was showcased to provide contextual intelligence and unlock operational efficiencies.

After the break the latest embedded AI scenarios in SAP S/4HANA were demonstrated, highlighting their impact on processes like Order to Cash, Warehouse Management, and Finance.

The session Accelerating Transformation with AI-Driven Custom Code Analysis
higlighted, how AI tools are streamlining custom code analysis and optimization during SAP GTS, EWM, and TM transformations, backed by real-world customer success stories.

In the session Enhancing the SAP Toolchain with AI attendees learned how SAP is infusing AI into its transformation toolchain—including LeanIX, Signavio, Cloud ALM, and Tricentis—to drive smarter process optimization, lifecycle management, and automated testing.

The final sessions showcased how Custom Innovation Services (CIS) empower organizations to co-innovate and build AI solutions tailored to their unique business needs and how SAP MaxAttention can support customers on each step of their AI Journey. The session also introduced the new AI Implementation Support service within SAP MaxAttention, designed to help customers move from AI experimentation to scalable, value-driven deployment.

Our Workshops

These sessions and workshop series focus on a specific topic and the purpose is to enable our customers to understand the product portfolio and innovative technologies of SAP in order to drive adoption and consumption. As specifically now, it is more important than ever to help companies to be successful and to respond to changing markets by transforming into intelligent enterprises in the experience economy.

Your feedback

Feel free to share your feedback and thoughts in the comment section below.

entry.title: SAP BTP Guidance Framework: A Compass for Architects, Developers, and Administrators
entry.content:
Say hello to the SAP BTP Guidance Framework—a practical framework tailored to anyone using the SAP Business Technology Platform. SAP BTP acts as the technological backbone for building, integrating, extending, and innovating with SAP solutions. Whether you're an enterprise architect, software developer, or administrator, SAP BTP offers significant opportunities for productivity and development.

Given the extensive options and tools SAP BTP offers, we created the SAP BTP Guidance Framework to help you fully leverage the platform. This framework serves as your compass, providing comprehensive guidance, simplifying your work, and helping you make smart technology choices.

The Guidance Framework provides clear direction and a structured approach to adopting SAP technologies. With this guidance, you can develop intelligent applications using advanced analytics, equipping your organization with a robust foundation to support agile innovation.

Let’s take a closer look at the main components:

Along your SAP BTP adoption journey, every choice makes a difference. Our Decision Guides help you identify the technology option that best fits your needs by providing navigation for architects, developers and administrators.

Which guides do we offer?

  • Integration architecture guide: supports you in evolving your existing integration architecture.
  • Extension architecture guide: supports the advancement of your existing extension architecture and helps you determine which SAP technology should be used in a given scenario.
  • SAP BTP Developer’s guide: improves the process of implementing a business application on SAP BTP.
  • SAP BTP Administrator’s guide: how- to set up your landscape and lifecycle management for running applications on SAP BTP.
  • SAP BTP Center of Expertise (CoE) guide: centralizing the strategy, governance and operating model to realize the potential of SAP BTP with the CoE, which has just recently moved to the SAP Guidance Framework.

If you are architecting solutions on SAP BTP, we offer curated solution templates. Our Reference Architectures are templates that outline consistent components and configurations for software implementations. They show how applications, data, and AI operate in both cloud and on-premises environments. These templates illustrate how different services interact and integrate with SAP, partner, and third-party applications. They provide a standardized approach that can be tailored to optimize systems and incorporate the latest cloud innovations.

To support this, we offer the SAP BTP Solution Diagram Repository on Draw.io, where you can create your own architectural landscapes. This tool allows you to visualize and extend your ideas, sparking inspiration and creativity, ideal for developers and architects working on future projects. You can find all of the available reference architectures in the new SAP Architecture Center.

When it comes to the know-how in SAP BTP, our Methodologies offer you a guided approach toward increasing your cloud technology maturity level, covering both technical and organizational aspects. As an architect, you might be interested in:

  • SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology: offers a framework that includes integration use case patterns, architecture blueprints, and additional best practices for cloud and hybrid IT landscapes. Its scope isn’t limited to technical aspects, but also covers the organizational dimension of enterprise integration like setting up an Integration Center of Excellence and defining integration roles including their tasks.
  • SAP Application Extension Methodology: provides a structured, technology-agnostic approach for customers and partners to define an organization-specific extension strategy. If you face complex challenges like ‘clean core’, ‘cloud readiness’, and ‘future proof’ concepts, you need to come up with a custom-tailored extension strategy for your organization. This methodology can help.
  • SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology: provides a comprehensive framework for enterprise and data architects to evaluate data strategies, develop solution architectures, and foster a data-driven culture in a structured four-phase approach.

Navigating decisions on your own can be tough, especially when it comes to security. To ensure secure technology implementation, we provide Recommendations for safe configurations. Developers and administrators can learn how to set up and monitor these security recommendations using SAP Cloud ALM. These guidelines assist in evaluating the security of SAP BTP configurations.

Discover DevOps with SAP BTP and learn about how these principles enable your development and operations teams to be more efficient. Especially for developers and administrators, this will be a helpful tool to optimize workflows and bring out the best in your team. You can browse through the capabilities and determine what fits best for you by filtering for DevOps in the SAP Discovery Center Service Catalog.

As you navigate the complex landscape of technology adoption, it’s clear that the right guidance can make all the difference. With the right resources, you can transform the challenge of technology adoption into an opportunity for growth and innovation. Whether you’re building from the ground up or fine-tuning an existing system, the SAP BTP Guidance Framework will help you move forward with confidence.

You Might Also be Interested In

The Guidance Framework is one of many learning and enablement resources when it comes to SAP BTP. If you’re interested in exploring other expert-curated resources, check out my other blog post, SAP BTP Resource Rundown, where you can find more information about other top assets and enablement sources.

Earlier this year the SAP BTP Guidance Framework was presented as a live YouTube series with role-based episodes. Take a look at the recordings:

  1. SAP BTP Guidance Framework Series: How-to Empower Architects, Developers, and Administrators
  2. SAP BTP Guidance Framework: How-to Empower Developers
  3. SAP BTP Guidance Framework: How-to Empower Administrators

Stay Tuned

This blog post is just the beginning of a deep dive into the SAP BTP Guidance Framework. Coming next will be additional blog posts covering the different topic areas, written by the experts themselves. Stay tuned!

entry.title: Re: Using EDI Envelopes in Integration Advisor
entry.content:
Hello Steve ( @Benko ),

If you export Runtime Artifacts for EDI Type Systems (like X12 or UN/EDIFACT), you will get 2 XSD files for two purposes.

The so-called MIG XSD (named <Your_MIG>_RD.xsd) describes the very specific message structure of your MIG with all the customizations you have done. This XSD allows you to perform a very detailed semantic validation if any payload corresponds to your MIG definition. Please check this XSD – this should include the Envelope segments.

Additionally, there is the so-called Message XSD (named like ASC-X12_850_004010.xsd). This XSD file is designed to be consumed in EDI Flow Steps (like EDI Splitter and EDI-To-XML Converter). These EDI Flow Steps need the Message XSD as input (and not the full Interchange XSD). This is the reason why the Envelope segments are not part of this XSD (by design!).

Feel free to have a look at this help page for more details how Integration Advisor artifacts are consumed: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/consuming-integration-advisor-artifacts-in-integration-flows .

I hope this answers your question.

Best regards,

Jörg

entry.title: Your opportunity to Influence SAP – Customer Engagement Initiative Projects 2025-2 for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
An exciting opportunity to influence SAP has started with the first cycle of the 2025 SAP Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI). The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you as an SAP customer or partner to get early insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions.

We are offering several interesting opportunities for customers to influence SAP’s S/4HANA solutions.

Find here the list of all projects that are relevant for the SAP S4/HANA Community or browse the whole project list here.

The projects will be open for customer registration until July 25, 2025.


SAP S4/HANA and SAP S4/HANA Cloud projects for registration:

 

ERP Cloud Delivery & Excellence

Potential Workforce Person Data Correction Tool

Test Data Variants–Driven Test Process Flow Control and Execution

Tool-Agnostic Test Automation from AI-Generated Process Variants for ERP Systems

 

ERP Digital Supply Chain Cloud

Empowering Reliability: Data-Driven Maintenance Efficiency Analysis for Strategy Optimization

Freight Rate Management for transportation processes with a low to medium complexity

Reimagine Warehouse Management with Joule

 

ERP Finance & Risk

AI assisted creation of balance validation rules AND comment proposal

Financial Reporting according to IFRS18 in S4HANA Cloud

Financial Statements in the New Review Booklets Analytical Application

 

ERP Private Cloud

Best Practice for Inspection Checklists in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud

 

ERP Public Cloud

Actual Liquidity Analysis

AI Agent for Open Item Clearing

AI-powered chatbot (Joule) for Intelligent Margin Analysis

Assess and improve your Quote-to-Cash process with meaningful Business Health KPIs

Enhancing Payment Run Experience

Optimizing Partner Solution Support in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Ecosystem

Price Recommendation in S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition

Research: Payment Advice In Existing Business Process

Sales Contract Management - Artificial Intelligent Handling for Quantity/Value Consumption by Joule

SAP Business Suite: Agility and Innovation through seamless Integration and Harmonization

Successor for Create and Process Non-Assigned Handling Units (HU02)

 

ERP User Experience

AI-assisted error resolution and data entry for SAP Fiori apps

AI-assisted Smart Visualization of MyHome for To-Dos (situations)

 

How do I register on the Customer Influence website? A short overview about how to find and register for a CEI project can be found on influence.sap.com.

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

Thank you in advance for your participation!

entry.title: SAP Community Team Spotlight: Moshe Naveh - June 2025 Edition
entry.content:
You might have seen our iterations of recognizing active authors (one / two / three / four / five / six)  in SAP Community. We also want to introduce the team behind the SAP Community platform. This month we want to introduce @moshenaveh to you which I interviewed recently. Read more about how he got started and what his day to day looks like behind the scenes of the SAP Community.

 

Interview with Moshe

Santos, Marco (MS): How did you first get involved with the SAP Community? What was your starting point?

Moshe Naveh: I started back in 2009 when I was hired to be part of the Community team, back then it was still called SCN. My main responsibility at the time was supporting wiki users and editors. That was my first major project, and it gave me a great opportunity to get to know the community deeply. I also supported various operational and engagement-related tasks, so from the beginning, I was fully immersed in the day-to-day of the community and its contributors.

MS: That sounds great, now that we talked about the past let’s switch to the present: can you tell us what your current role on the SAP Community team is, and what a typical day looks like for you?

Moshe Naveh: I’m part of the SAP Community team, and my role is very diverse. A big part of my work is supporting community members across multiple channels. Whether it’s through the Community inbox, internal systems, or online discussions. I help identify different types of needs, like content, training, or even platform-related issues, and I work to address those in a way that benefits our users and stakeholders. 

My day usually starts with checking in on those support channels and then moves into daily moderation tasks. I also meet regularly with stakeholders and SAP Community collaborators, helping them understand how the community can support their goals. That includes coaching them on features, best practices, helping topic page editors with their contributions and how to increase engagement in the SAP Community.

I’m a people person, and I think that’s why I joined this team in the first place. It’s all about engagement and collaboration. On top of that, I’m constantly thinking of ways to improve the experience on the SAP Community and make it more enjoyable, whether that’s through challenges, engaging threads, or other interactive initiatives. One of the proudest moments for me was launching the "Blog It Forward" initiative.

Here are some examples:

What is your favorite dish or snack?

My favorite place is ... because...

Food for thought: What’s your favorite holiday/celebration dish?

 MS: That sounds great. What’s one part of the whole community experience that you're especially proud of contributing to?

Moshe Naveh: It’s hard to choose just one thing, but what really stands out to me is the day-to-day support I provide to community members and encourage them to engage. A great example is a very interesting blog series by SAP Sapphire speakers. I genuinely enjoy interacting with them, whether it’s through the community inbox or other channels and listening to their needs. Helping them overcome challenges they face and seeing their positive feedback afterward really warms my heart. Those moments of connection and appreciation are what keep me going and remind me why this work matters so much.

MS: You have mentioned before that you have a very memorable moment or time that you remember very fondly in the community. Do you want to elaborate a little bit on that?

Moshe Naveh: Yes, absolutely. Back in 2012, I was out for a jog. Funny enough, that’s often when my creativity kicks in. I was thinking about new ways to engage people in the community, and that’s when the idea for Blog It Forward came to me. It was a chain of blog posts where one person would write about themselves using a set of suggested questions, include some personal photos, and then “forward” the challenge to others. It quickly grew into a global storytelling movement with hundreds of participants. Even today, people remember it as one of the most enjoyable initiatives we’ve had and I’m incredibly proud to have sparked that.

MS: Can you elaborate a little bit on how you stay in touch with community needs and feedback?

Moshe Naveh: Sure. There are multiple ways we stay connected with the community. We regularly monitor our discussion forums and content areas to see what’s being posted. We also hold stakeholder meetings and participate in community calls where people share their thoughts directly. Many members reach out to us via email or internal channels, but the best way to contact the team is through the community@sap.com inbox. That’s where we gather a lot of insights and feedback. It helps us understand what members need and how we can support them better.

MS: If there’s one thing that you would really love to see more of in the community, what would that be?

Moshe Naveh: I always enjoy seeing and meeting new people in the community. Every new member brings their own qualities, experiences, and contributions, and that enriches the entire community. So, I’d love to see more new members joining. Creating a welcoming and inclusive environment is really close to my heart—that’s something we focused on in the Blog It Forward initiative and in other engaging threads. It’s also why I recently shared a blog post on using alternative text to make community content more accessible and inclusive.

MS: Great to hear that. And now we're at the last question of the interview: is there anything else that you would like to share?

Moshe Naveh: I think it's often a challenge for people to take that first step and start engaging in the community. But once they do, everything becomes easier. So I encourage everyone to just say hi, explore the different ways of participating, share your knowledge, and grow together with others. It’s truly a journey we can all take together—and it’s one that’s definitely worth it. 

MS: This is a  great message to end the interview. Thank you so much for joining today and sharing your experiences with the community!

entry.title: SAP Job Scheduling Service: Migrate jobs & schedules from one instance to another
entry.content:
SAP Job Scheduling Service: Migrate jobs & schedules from one instance to another

Do you need to migrate jobs and schedules from one service instance to another — for example, from a development environment to production? Most probably yes.

Is there automatic way to do that? Currently - no ☹️, but we can do it using the existing REST API endpoints of the Job Scheduling service.

In this post, we’ll walk through a Node.js script that automates this migration process for HTTP Endpoint jobs. This is especially useful when you want to replicate your job configurations across environments without manually recreating them.

⚠️ Notes: 

  • This is NOT a migration tool - it is just an example how to use existing REST APIs to migrate jobs and schedules
  • ✅This script only supports HTTP Endpoint jobs. 
  • ❌It does not support CF Tasks. (CF Tasks cannot be managed with the REST API)
  • 🔁It assumes that application routes/endpoints remain unchanged between environments.
  • 🖥️ It assumes that applications are configured to work with the new Job Scheduling service instance (applications are already migrated)

📦Prerequisites

Before you begin, make sure you have:

  • Two SAP Job Scheduler service instances (source and target)
  • Service keys for both instances saved as export-service-key.txt and import-service-key.txt from the BTP Cockpit
  • Node.js and npm installed
  • Basic familiarity with JavaScript and SAP BTP

🛠️ What the Script Does

The script performs the following steps:

  1. Authenticates with both the source and target Job Scheduling service instances using OAuth2.
  2. Retrieves all HTTP Endpoint jobs from the source instance.
  3. For each job, fetches its full definition including schedules.
  4. Cleans the job data by removing system-assigned fields like jobId and scheduleId.
  5. Recreates the job and its schedules in the target instance.

Creating service-keys

We need 2 service keys for the two instances so that we can "talk" to them. We can create these service keys either in the cockpit or using the CLI.

We can name them export-service-key for the source instance and import-service-key for the service instance where we want to "import" the data, the target.

You need to download them and place them in the same folder as the script.

 

💻The Code

You should have the following structure:

job-scheduling-service-rest-migrator/
├── index.js                 # Main script using ES module syntax
├── package.json             # Project metadata and dependencies
├── export-service-key.txt   # Service key for source service instance
├── import-service-key.txt   # Service key for target service instance
 

package.json

{
  "name": "job-scheduling-service-rest-migrator",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Used to migrate jobs and schedules from one instance to another using the REST API",
  "main": "index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "start": "node index.js"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "axios": "^1.9.0",
    "qs": "^6.14.0"
  }
}
 

index.js

import fs from 'node:fs';
import axios from 'axios';
import qs from 'qs';


// 🔐 Load Service Keys
// 🔑 Get OAuth Tokens for the two service instances
// 📥 Fetch All Jobs from Source (Export) Instance
// 🔍 Fetch Job Details with Schedules
// 🧹 Clean Job Data
// 📤 Post Job to Target (Import) Instance
// 🚀 Main Migration Function

// 🔐========================= LOAD SERVICE KEYS =========================🔐
const exportServiceKey = JSON.parse(
  fs.readFileSync("export-service-key.txt", "utf8")
);

const importServiceKey = JSON.parse(
  fs.readFileSync("import-service-key.txt", "utf8")
);

// 🔑========================= AUTHENTICATION ============================🔑
// https://help.sap.com/docs/job-scheduling/sap-job-scheduling-service/authentication#service-plan-standard
async function getAccessToken(serviceKey) {
  const tokenUrl = `${serviceKey.uaa.url}/oauth/token`;
  const data = qs.stringify({
    grant_type: "client_credentials",
    client_id: serviceKey.uaa.clientid,
    client_secret: serviceKey.uaa.clientsecret,
  });

  const response = await axios.post(tokenUrl, data, {
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
    },
  });
  console.log(`🔑 Successfully fetched oAuth token from '${tokenUrl}'`);
  return response.data.access_token;
}

// 📥========================= RETRIEVE JOBS ================================📥
// https://help.sap.com/docs/job-scheduling/sap-job-scheduling-service/retrieve-jobs
async function getAllJobs(token) {
  const response = await axios.get(`${exportServiceKey.url}/scheduler/jobs?jobType=HTTP_ENDPOINT`, {
    headers: {
      Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
    },
  });

  return response.data;
}

// 🔍========================= RETRIEVE JOB DETAILS =========================🔍
// https://help.sap.com/docs/job-scheduling/sap-job-scheduling-service/retrieve-job-schedule-details
async function getJob(jobId, token) {
  const response = await axios.get(
    `${exportServiceKey.url}/scheduler/jobs/${jobId}?displaySchedules=true`,
    {
      headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` },
    }
  );
  return response.data;
}

// 🧹========================= CLEAN JOB DATA ============================🧹
function removeScheduleIdsFromJob(job) {
  // Remove fields that shouldn't be copied
  if (Array.isArray(job.schedules)) {
    job.schedules = job.schedules.map(({ scheduleId, ...rest }) => rest);
  }
  return job;
}

// 📤========================= CREATE JOB IN TARGET ========================📤
// https://help.sap.com/docs/job-scheduling/sap-job-scheduling-service/create-job
async function postJobToImportInstance(job, token, serviceKey) {
  console.log(`📦 Migrating job: '${job.name}' to instance: '${serviceKey.uaa.serviceInstanceId}'`);
  try {
    const response = await axios.post(
      `${serviceKey.url}/scheduler/jobs`,
      job,
      {
        headers: {
          Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`,
          "Content-Type": "application/json",
        },
      }
    );
    console.log(`✅ Job '${job.name}' imported successfully.`);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(
      `❌ Failed to import job '${job.name}':`,
      error.response?.data || error.message
    );
  }
}

// 🚀========================= MIGRATION PROCESS =========================🚀
async function migrateJobs() {
  const exportToken = await getAccessToken(exportServiceKey);
  const importToken = await getAccessToken(importServiceKey);

  const jobsResponse = await getAllJobs(exportToken);
  console.log(`⚙️ Number of jobs retrieved: '${jobsResponse.total}' from service instance ☁️ '${exportServiceKey.uaa.serviceInstanceId}'`);

  for (const job of jobsResponse.results) {
    const jobWithSchedules = await getJob(job.jobId, exportToken);
	// Let the system assign a new ID
	delete jobWithSchedules.jobId;
    const cleanedJobs = removeScheduleIdsFromJob(jobWithSchedules);
    
    await postJobToImportInstance(cleanedJobs, importToken, importServiceKey);
  }
}

// 🏁========================= START SCRIPT ==============================🏁
migrateJobs().catch(console.error);
 

🚀Running the Script

Note Ensure export-service-key.txt and import-service-key.txt are in the same directory as the script.

Install dependencies

npm install

Run the index.js

npm run start

You’ll see logs for each job being migrated, including success or error messages.

 

Terminal Output of Script Execution

✅Conclusion

Migrating jobs between SAP Job Scheduler instances doesn’t have to be a manual process. With a bit of scripting, you can automate the entire workflow and ensure consistency across environments.

This approach is especially helpful in CI/CD pipelines, when managing multiple BTP subaccounts, or when you want to migrate from one datacenter to another.

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing Learning
entry.content:
Are you searching for content to kickstart your learning journey in SAP Digital Manufacturing?

In this blog post, I've compiled a comprehensive list of resources to help you familiarize yourself with the latest cloud-based MES offerings from SAP.

But first, what is SAP Digital Manufacturing?

SAP Digital Manufacturing is an enterprise-level, scalable, manufacturing solution that enables global manufacturers to manage and control manufacturing and shop floor operations and realize Industry 4.0 scenarios.

It’s a cloud-based Manufacturing Execution System (MES) that supports sustainable, risk-resilient manufacturing operations through a resource-efficient Industry 4.0 approach, enabling companies to integrate and embed intelligence in manufacturing processes.

It provides coordination of planning and execution processes, covering all aspects of the manufacturing cycle from planning to the shop floor and beyond.

Now, how can you learn it?

Discovering SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course is a comprehensive module aimed at providing an in-depth understanding of the placement, applicability, and integration possibilities of SAP Digital Manufacturing within the manufacturing vertical structure.

Analyzing Manufacturing Data in SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course aims at business users and consultants, who want to analyze the manufacturing data. The reporting options in SAP Digital Manufacturing are introduced which ensure traceability and generate insights to understand the root-causes of losses on the shop floor.

Configuring SAP Digital Manufacturing for Execution: Basic Data and Configuration

This enablement aims business users, consultants, solution architects, and industry specialists, who want to configure, execute, and control shop floor processes in the discrete manufacturing area using SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Executing Processes for Discrete Industries in SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course aims at business users and consultants, who want to execute shopfloor control processes in the discrete manufacturing area using SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Executing Processes for Process Industries in SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course is aimed at business users and consultants who want to execute shop floor control processes in the process manufacturing area using SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Implementing Machine Integration in SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course provides a comprehensive deep-dive into understanding and configuring the shop floor integration with SAP Digital Manufacturing to enable machine connectivity

Configuring Machine Integration in SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course provides the IT fundamentals of setting up and connecting the on-premise applications SAP Production Connector and SAP Cloud Connector required for machine connectivity with the cloud application SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Exploring Customization in SAP Digital Manufacturing

This course aims consultants, solution architects and industry specialists, who want to explore more about the extensibility mechanisms in SAP Digital Manufacturing. The course will especially focus on the Production Operator Dashboard Designer and the Production Process Designer.

You can also check SAP Digital Manufacturing IVJ Playlist below:

* Better viewed in Desktop or iPad in landscape mode

Hope it helps and inspires you!
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Joule based SAP HANA Application Migration Assistant - Transforming with Gen AI
entry.content:
In Early 2024, released first version of the SAP Business Application Studio extension which  Automates conversion of SAP HANA Database artifacts to SAP HANA Cloud artifacts.   SAP HANA  database objects (such as .hdbcds, .hdbtable, .hdbview, etc.) are transformed into their corresponding SAP CAP compliant database artifacts (e.g., .cds models).

The Q2-2025  release , enables service layer migration using GenAI capabilities in SAP Build Code. The assistant analyses SAP XS objects - xsodata ,xsjslib, xsjs and  files and generate corresponding service.cds, service.js and custom handler implementations under the CAP service layer. These AI capabilities  accelerates the migration of service layer while preserving structure and functional behaviour where possible. A sample conversion of xsodata to SAP CAP service definition is shown below. 

poWorklist.xsodata

service namespace "com.sap.openSAP.hana5.example.service"  { 
  "sap.hana.democontent.epm.models::PO_HEADER_EN" as "PO_WORKLIST" 
   keys ("PURCHASEORDERID","PURCHASEORDERITEM");
   
   "sap.hana.democontent.epm.models::PO_ITEM" as "PurchaseOrderItem" keys ("PURCHASEORDERID","PURCHASEORDERITEM");
   
   "PO.HeaderView" as "PurchaseOrderHeader" keys("PurchaseOrderId")
   navigates (     
        "PurchaseOrderHeader_PurchaseOrderItem" as "PurchaseOrderItems" from principal);

   association "PurchaseOrderHeader_PurchaseOrderItem"
      principal "PurchaseOrderHeader"("PurchaseOrderId") multiplicity "1"
      dependent "PurchaseOrderItem"("PurchaseOrderId") multiplicity "*";

}

service.cds

//converted from: /users/projects/hana-shine-xsa/core-xsjs/lib/xsodata/poWorklist.xsodata
service poWorklist @(path:'/poWorklist') {
    entity PO_WORKLIST as projection on SAP_HANA_DEMOCONTENT_EPM_MODELS_PO_HEADER_EN {
        *,
        PurchaseOrderItems: association to many PurchaseOrderItem on PurchaseOrderItems.PurchaseOrderId = $self.PURCHASEORDERID
    };

    entity PurchaseOrderItem as projection on SAP_HANA_DEMOCONTENT_EPM_MODELS_PO_ITEM;

    entity PurchaseOrderHeader as projection on PO.HeaderView {
        *,
        PurchaseOrderItems: association to many PurchaseOrderItem on PurchaseOrderItems.PurchaseOrderId = $self.PurchaseOrderId
    };
}

The converted code includes comments linking back to the original source files - making it easy for users to verify that key business logic has been accurately transformed.  In the similar way, all xsjslib and xsjs files are re-written as SAP CAP custom handlers. The resulting  modularised SAP CAP compliant project mirrors the original directory structure, ensuring familiarity and easy traceability.  All SAP HANA objects  will be adjusted automatically to follow SAP HANA Cloud naming conventions.

Additionally, the assistant generates a detailed report outlining recommended post-migration steps to guide users through final adjustments

As generative AI conversion is not guaranteed to be 100% accurate, human intervention is required post-migration to validate, refine, and productionize the application logic and service definitions.  

References

  • For prerequisites and step by step guide refer to corresponding xsc-to-cap  and xsa-to-cap repositories. 
  • Help Document: Migrating XS Classic Applications to SAP CAP
  • Help Document: SAP HANA XS Advanced Migration Guide

entry.title: Re: S4/HANA Cloud - How to block Customers and Supplier f...
entry.content:
Hello  @marceloromeiro 

The ODATA V2 API API_BUSINESS_PARTNER is the correct and supported option for this in Public Cloud. It provides access to the necessary blocking fields at both the Customer and Supplier levels, including

  • CompanyCodeData.BlockingIndicator (Customer)
  • SupplierCompany.BlockingIndicator or PurchasingOrg.PurchasingBlock (Supplier)

Reference

API_BUSINESS_PARTNER – SAP API Hub

No standalone Business Object provides that functionality directly in the Public Cloud.

With kind regard

Chuma

entry.title: Override Booking Text in FAGLF101 and F.50
entry.content:
Dear experts,

is it possible to override the system-defined posting text (ACDOCA-SGTXT/BSEG-SGTXT) for transactions F.50 and FAGLF101? I don't think this is possible in standard Customizing, but perhaps there's an exit or something similar?

Thanks and best regards
Markus

entry.title: Re: xercesc_3_2::RuntimeException Error while loading SAP...
entry.content:
What SP of CR for VS are you using?

You can get the latest from here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

Check the platforms support info also for supported OS's etc.

See my blog on upgrading your project:

Upgrading a Visual Studio 2008->2022 .NET project with the latest CR .NET SDK packages

Also note CR for VS runtime will only be 64 at the end of this year, time to upgrade your project 64 bit.
